definition: The USD creates either self-correcting or self-reinforcing commodity-inflation dynamics depending on Fed credibility: Fed ahead of curve → dollar strengthens → commodities moderate (self-correcting); Fed behind curve → real rates fall → dollar weakens → commodities accelerate (self-reinforcing).
evidence: 2022 self-correcting: aggressive Fed tightening strengthened dollar, helping moderate commodity prices
1970s self-reinforcing: negative real rates weakened dollar, amplifying commodity inflation
Regime identification depends on whether Fed is perceived as ahead or behind the inflation curve
open_questions: What real-time indicators best identify which loop is operating?
Is there a tipping point between self-correcting and self-reinforcing dynamics?
How does fiscal dominance risk affect the loop identification?
